body_txt: It's good to eat well. Your body needs the right balance of vitamins and nutrients for many reasons, and it's also good to stay in shape. Sometimes, a person sticks to an all-natural diet of their own choice for these reasons. But sometimes, the person takes it too far by not only sticking to a diet but obsessing over whether everything they eat is healthy or nutritious, even going so far as to police other people's eating habits (which may result in If It Tastes Bad, It Must Be Good for You ) if they get fanatical enough about it! (Downplayed cases often involve parents making sure their kids are eating well.) As such, a Meat Versus Veggies debate often pops up. Worth noting that in Real Life , people with an excessive preoccupation with eating healthy food to the point of disordered eating and malnourishment (i.e., lack of carbs) are noted to be diagnosed with orthorexia nervosa . This is a proposed eating disorder that is not yet officially recognized by any major psychological society or diagnostic manual, though this is mostly a question of the slowness of the system and not necessarily a comment on the validity of the diagnosis (which was first proposed in 1997 and only attracted significant research starting in the late 2000s to early 2010s). Tends to be a common trait of the Tech Bro . May overlap with Fitness Nut if the character's health obsession extends to doing excessive sports/exercise as well. Sister trope to Straw Vegetarian and maybe Granola Girl , who are likely to eat a health food-type diet but may do so more for ethical or cultural reasons than out of concern for their health. Compare Hypochondriac and Weight Woe for other cases of people being very worried about their health and/or weight. Might be the reason behind a Plain Palate . Also has overlap with Smug Straight Edge .
